content.verdict: Time + Tide Miavana is the most ambitious private-island lodge built in Africa this century — 14 free-standing residences scattered across Nosy Ankao, a 6km uninhabited islet 9km off Madagascar's far northeastern tip, accessed exclusively by a 45-minute helicopter scenic flight from Diego Suarez (formerly Antsiranana). Designed by Silvio Rech and Lesley Carstens (the architects behind North Island in Seychelles), Miavana is what happens when the most experienced safari operator in Zambia (Norman Carr's family, now Time + Tide) decides to build a marine sanctuary at scale: every villa has its own pool, butler, and fitness equipment; the spa occupies its own beach cove; and a resident marine biologist runs the world-class diving and turtle conservation programme. For honeymoon couples in a position to spend genuinely without limit, Miavana delivers the rarest combination on earth — true private-island seclusion with full-service infrastructure, a marine reserve as your front yard, and the wildest coastline in the Indian Ocean as your context. The endemic-species premise is real: humpback whales calve in these waters July–October, dugongs graze the seagrass, and the resident sifakas of nearby Nosy Hara reserve are visible on a 20-minute helicopter excursion.
content.best_room: All 14 Ocean Beach Villas are essentially identical — 540m² of poured-concrete-and-glass architecture, 12m private pool, two outdoor showers, indoor-outdoor lounge, two butler-served kitchens, and direct beach steps. Villa 1 and Villa 2 anchor the southern beach with the longest sand walks; Villa 13 and Villa 14 sit at the wild northern point for sunrise drama. Couples typically request Villa 7 (central, panoramic) or Villa 13 (most isolated). The four-bedroom Miavana Residence is a private compound for a maximum honeymoon take-over.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Antananarivo Arrival & Onward
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land at Ivato (TNR), connect same-day on Tsaradia (Madagascar Airlines) 90 minutes north to Diego Suarez (Antsiranana), where Miavana's ground team meets you. Overnight at the Allamanda Hotel for the connecting transit. Light dinner of grilled rougeret fish and a first Three Horses Beer on the colonial veranda — a soft landing before the helicopter.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Helicopter to Nosy Ankao
itinerary_7_nights.description: Miavana's H125 helicopter lifts off from Diego at 09:00 — a 45-minute scenic flight banking past the Tsingy Rouge red sandstone formations, over the Mozambique Channel, with the first glimpse of Nosy Ankao's white sand bar an unreal moment. Land at the lodge pad, butler greeting, and into your villa. Private lunch on the deck, swim, snorkel from the beach, and an early sunset cocktail at the Sand Bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lemur Excursion & Reef Dive
itinerary_7_nights.description: Helicopter excursion (40 minutes) to Nosy Hara National Park to track Madagascar's smallest primate — the Microcebus mouse lemur — and the endemic crowned lemur troops, with the resident naturalist. Picnic lunch on a private sandbar, return to Miavana for an afternoon dive on the Cape Amber reef wall (28°C water, 30m visibility, sea turtles guaranteed).

itinerary_7_nights.title: Snorkel, Kitesurf, Whale Watch
itinerary_7_nights.description: A water-led day — morning snorkel from the lodge boat to the offshore bommie cluster (octopus, parrotfish, blacktip reef sharks), midday kitesurf lesson on the protected lagoon (the steady 18-knot trade wind is perfect for beginners), and in season (Jul–Oct) a 90-minute humpback-whale encounter — Miavana operates ethical, single-vessel viewing only.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Antsiranana Bay Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Helicopter into Antsiranana (35 minutes) for a guided morning at the colonial-era market — vanilla pods, cloves, baobab seeds — and a traditional Malagasy lunch of romazava beef stew at La Rosticceria. Return via the Emerald Sea (Mer d'Émeraude), landing on a sandbar for an afternoon of snorkeling in 1m of glass-clear water. Back at Miavana for spa treatments before dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Baobab Avenue & Private Sandbar
itinerary_7_nights.description: An ambitious dawn helicopter to the Baobab Avenue micro-stand in Mahamasina (90 minutes each way) — Miavana arranges this rarely-offered trip for couples who specifically request the iconic baobab silhouette photo at sunrise. Picnic breakfast amongst the giants, return via the Tsingy Rouge canyon for an aerial pass. Afternoon: a private sandbar drop with champagne and lobster, collected at sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Dive & Helicopter Out
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final morning dive (or sleep-in) and a long final breakfast on your villa terrace. The 14:00 helicopter back to Diego connects the 16:30 Tsaradia to Tana, arriving in time for the 22:00 Air France or Air Mauritius flight back to Europe. Miavana handles every leg of the connection — a single bill for the entire week, single butler arranging every detail.

caveats: Access is helicopter-only from Diego Suarez (Antsiranana) — there is no boat transfer option, and the helicopter is weather-dependent; a 24-hour buffer either side of the lodge is non-negotiable for international connections.

Madagascar requires antimalarial prophylaxis (Malarone or doxycycline) — Nosy Ankao is offshore and lower-risk than the mainland, but standard advice is to take prophylaxis throughout and consult a travel clinic 6 weeks ahead.

Miavana is direct-booking-only via reservations@miavana.com or through Time + Tide's preferred agent network (Cazenove+Loyd, Original Travel, Audley) — there is no OTA inventory whatsoever and no published rate sheet; expect $4,500–9,000 per villa per night all-inclusive.
Internet is satellite-based and intentionally limited — adequate for email but not for video calls or streaming; the lodge positions this as part of the experience.
Cyclone season (Jan–March) brings genuine weather and access risk — the lodge typically closes mid-Feb through mid-March; honeymoon timing is best May–November.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Miavana is the most sophisticated private-island honeymoon in the Indian Ocean — 14 villas across an entire 6km islet means you can spend a full day without seeing another guest. The combination of private pool, butler, helicopter excursions, marine biologist programme, and Time + Tide service standards is unmatched outside of North Island in the Seychelles. For couples who want the wildest, most secluded honeymoon possible, this is the answer.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May through November is dry season with the most reliable conditions. July through October is the humpback whale calving season — a genuine highlight. December and April are pleasant shoulders. Avoid mid-January through mid-March: cyclone season, lodge typically closed.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: Miavana operates at most 28 guests across the island and books 9–12 months ahead for honeymoon high season (Jul–Oct, Christmas, Easter). Villa 7 (central panoramic) and Villa 13 (northern point) are the most-requested. The Miavana Residence (4 bedrooms, private pool compound) requires 12 months notice for full take-over.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Miavana welcomes families with children over 6 but the all-villa configuration and high price point mean honeymoon couples and milestone-anniversary couples dominate the guest mix. Villas are spaced 100m+ apart with full visual privacy.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: All 14 Ocean Beach Villas are functionally identical — choose by position. Villa 7 for a central, panoramic outlook; Villa 13 for the most isolated sunrise wing; Villa 1 for the longest beach walk southward. The Miavana Residence (4 bedrooms) is a private compound for take-over honeymoons or multi-generation buy-outs.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to Antananarivo (TNR) via Paris (Air France 11h direct), Mauritius (Air Mauritius), Addis Ababa (Ethiopian), or Nairobi (Kenya Airways). Connect on Tsaradia (Madagascar Airlines) 90 minutes to Diego Suarez (Antsiranana, DIE). Miavana's H125 helicopter (45 minutes, included) lands on the lodge's pad. Total door-to-door from Europe is 36 hours including the obligatory transit night in Tana.

content.email_template:

Dear Miavana reservations,

We are planning our honeymoon for [DATE]–[DATE] (7 nights) and are interested in an Ocean Beach Villa (ideally Villa 7 or Villa 13). Please advise on availability, helicopter transfer schedule from Diego Suarez, honeymoon inclusions, and whale-encounter availability if travelling in season.

Thank you,
[Your names]
